title: Checkout recovery under database uncertainty
business_context: A fictional commerce platform is entering a high-volume ordering window when checkout failures and inconsistent order writes appear together.
initial_stakes: Checkout errors exceed the operating threshold, order consistency is uncertain, and dependent contracts have measurable hourly exposure.

turns.situation: Checkout workers are failing while database writer ownership remains uncertain.

options.label: Restart checkout service
options.description: Recycle checkout workers before validating the database writer.
options.expected_outcome: Retry traffic expands the dependency cascade.

options.label: Freeze writes and identify primary database writer
options.description: Pause risky writes and verify writer ownership.
options.expected_outcome: Order consistency is protected while recovery evidence is collected.

turns.situation: Retry volume is elevated and the database team needs a safe containment decision.

options.label: Freeze writes and identify primary database writer
options.description: Stop conflicting writes and validate replication health.
options.expected_outcome: The incident stabilizes around a verified writer.

options.label: Fail over database immediately
options.description: Force failover without validating replication state.

turns.situation: Containment is improving, but ownership and stakeholder checkpoints are fragmented.

options.label: Escalate to database lead and incident command
options.description: Assign decision owners and establish a shared checkpoint cadence.
options.expected_outcome: Recovery ownership and communications become explicit.

options.label: Continue without incident command
options.description: Keep recovery inside the service team.
options.expected_outcome: Technical work continues with coordination risk.

turns.situation: Error rates are declining, but the team must prove the fault domain before restoration.

options.label: Correlate traces across checkout, orders, and database
options.description: Use telemetry to validate recovery state across dependencies.
options.expected_outcome: The fault domain and recovery evidence become clear.

options.label: Restore all traffic now
options.description: Reopen traffic before consistency evidence is complete.
options.expected_outcome: Customer traffic returns before recovery controls are proven.

turns.situation: Consistency checks pass and the team must choose the restoration pattern.

options.label: Restore traffic gradually after consistency checks
options.description: Reopen traffic in stages with checkpoints.
options.expected_outcome: Customer traffic returns while residual risk remains controlled.

options.label: Remove all traffic controls
options.expected_outcome: The platform resumes full load without staged validation.
